questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>γ</mi></math> are the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then find <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>∑</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></math>
questions.solution:
x3-2x2+3x-4=0 αβγ

α+β+γ=2

αβγ=4, ∑αβ=3

αβ(α+β)+βγ(β+γ)+γα(γ+α)

αβ(2-γ)+βγ(2-α)+γα(2-β)

=2∑αβ-3αβγ

=2(3)-3×4

=-6
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of the equation $x^2+b x+c=0$ and $\alpha+h, \beta+h$ are the roots of the equation $x^2+q x+r=0$, then $h$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation
$\begin{array}{r}x^2+b x+c=0 \\ \alpha+\beta=-b \text { and } \alpha \beta=c\end{array}$
Also $\alpha+h$ and $\beta+h$ are the roots of the equations
We have,
$x^2+q x+r=0$
$\begin{array}{rlrl} & \therefore & \alpha+h+\beta+h & =-q \\ -b+2 h & =-q \\ \Rightarrow & h & =\frac{(b-q)}{2}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
l=\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{3 \sin \theta-4 \sin ^2 \theta}{\theta}\right]
$$
Using $L$-hospital's rule
$$
=\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{3 \cos \theta-8 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{1}=3
$$
and
$$
\begin{aligned}
m & =\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \tan \theta}{\theta\left(1-\tan ^2 \theta\right)} \\
& =\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\tan 2 \theta}{\theta} \\
& =\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\tan 2 \theta}{2 \theta} \cdot 2=2
\end{aligned}
$$
The required quadratic equation is
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2-(l+m) x+l m=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x^2-(3+2) x+3 \cdot 2=0 \\
& x^2-5 x+6=0 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The remainder obtained by dividing the polynomial x<sup>100</sup> by the polynomial x<sup>2</sup> - 3x + 2 is equal to
questions.solution:
Concept Involved
Remainder Theorem  If a polynomial P(x) is divided by x - α , then the remainder obtained is equal to p α .

Dividend x100
Divisor   x2 - 3x + 2 = (x - 1) (x - 2)

Let P(x) = x100

            = (x - 1) (x - 2) Q(x) + R(x)                    ...(i)

Since, the divisor is a quadratic, hence the degree of remainder should be less than 2 (i.e., ≤  1).
Let us assume that the remainder obtained is R(x) = ax + b, then to find R(x), we have to basically find the values of a and b.
On substituting x = 1 in Eq. (i) we get

         P(1) = 1100 = (1 - 1) (x - 2) Q (x) + R(1)

⇒     R(1) = 1

⇒     a + b = 1                                             ...(ii)

On substituting x = 2 in Eq. (i) we get

         P(2) = 2100 = (2 - 1) (2 - 2) Q (x) + R(2)

⇒     R(2) = 2100

⇒     2a + b = 2100                                  ...(iii)

On solving Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get

                   a = 2200 - 1

and             b = 2 - 2100

Hence, the remainder obtained is

         R(x) = ax + b = (2100 - 1) x + (2 - 2100)

⇒     R(x) = (2100 - 1) x - 2(299 - 1)

Alternatively

When x100 is divided by x - 1, then the remainder obtained is 1100 .

⇒    x 1 0 0 x - 1 = q 1 x + 1 x - 1                             ...(i)

Similarly, when x100 is divided by x - 2, the remainder obtained is 2100.

⇒    x 1 0 0 x - 2 = q 2 x + 2 1 0 0 x - 2                          ...(ii)

On subtracting Eq. (ii) from Eq. (i), we get

     x 1 0 0 x - 1 - x 1 0 0 x - 2 = q 1 x - q 2 x + 1 x - 1 - 2 1 0 0 x - 2

⇒    - x 1 0 0 x - 1 x - 2 = q 1 x - q 2 x + 1 x - 1 - 2 1 0 0 x - 2

⇒    - x 1 0 0 x - 1 x - 2 = q 2 x - q 1 x + 2 1 0 0 x - 1 - x - 2 x - 1 x - 2

∴    Remainder = 2100(x - 1) - (x - 2)
                         = (2100 - 1) x - 2(299 - 1)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The minimum value of $(x-\alpha)(x-\beta)$ is
questions.solution:
Let $y=(x-\alpha)(x-\beta)$
$\begin{aligned} y & =x^2-(\alpha+\beta) x+\alpha \beta \\ y^{\prime} & =2 x-(\alpha+\beta)\end{aligned}$
For maximum or minimum, put $y^{\prime}=0$
$\begin{array}{rrrl}\Rightarrow & 2 x-(\alpha+\beta) & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x & =\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & y^{\prime \prime} & =2=+ \text { ve }\end{array}$
The minimum value at $x=\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}$ is
$\begin{aligned} y & =\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}-\alpha\right)\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}-\beta\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{4}(\beta-\alpha)(\alpha-\beta)=-\frac{1}{4}(\alpha-\beta)^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the real roots of the equation
$\sqrt{\frac{5 x}{x-2}}+\sqrt{\frac{x-2}{5 x}}=\frac{29}{10}$ and $\alpha>\beta$, then $\sqrt{\alpha^2-11^4 \beta^2}=$
questions.solution:
We have, $\sqrt{\frac{5 x}{x-2}}+\sqrt{\frac{x-2}{5 x}}=\frac{29}{10}$
Let $\quad \sqrt{\frac{5 x}{x-2}}=y$
Then, $y+\frac{1}{y}=\frac{29}{10} \Rightarrow \frac{y^2+1}{y}=\frac{29}{10}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 10 y^2+10=29 y$
$\Rightarrow \quad 10 y^2-29 y+10=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad 10 y^2-25 y-4 y+10=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad 5 y(2 y-5)-2(2 y-5)=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad(5 y-2)(2 y-5)=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=\frac{2}{5}, \frac{5}{2}$
Now, if $y=\frac{2}{5} \Rightarrow \sqrt{\frac{5 x}{x-2}}=\frac{2}{5}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{5 x}{x-2}=\frac{4}{25}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 125 x=4 x-8$
$\Rightarrow \quad 121 x=-8$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=-8 / 121$
And if $y=\frac{5}{2} \Rightarrow \sqrt{\frac{5 x}{x-2}}=\frac{5}{2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{5 x}{x-2}=\frac{25}{4}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{x-2}=\frac{5}{4} \Rightarrow 5 x-10=4 x$
$\Rightarrow \quad x=10$
$\therefore$ Let $\alpha=10$ and $\beta=\frac{-8}{121}$ $(\because \alpha>\beta)$
$\therefore \quad \sqrt{\alpha^2-11^4 \beta^2}$
$=\sqrt{(10)^2-11^4\left(\frac{-8}{121}\right)^2}$
$=\sqrt{100-64}$
$=\sqrt{36}=6$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></math> being consecutive integers and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>D</mi></msqrt></math> is
questions.solution:
We have,

D=a2+b2+c2

Since, a and b are consecutive integers therefore, b=a+1 and c=ab=aa+1. Then

D=a2+b2+c2

  =a2+a+12+a2a+12

=a21+a+12+a+12

=a2a2+2a+2+a2+2a+1

=a4+2a3+3a2+2a+1

=a2+a+12

∴ D=aa+1+1= an odd integer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If $\frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x-2 k}=\frac{2}{x-2}-\frac{1}{x+k^{\prime}}$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned} \frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x-2 k} & =\frac{2}{x-2}-\frac{1}{x+k} \\ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x-4}{x^2-5 x-2 k} & =\frac{2(x+k)-(x-2)}{(x-2)(x+k)} \\ & =\frac{x+2 k+2}{(x-2)(x+k)}\end{aligned}$
On equating, we get
$\begin{aligned}-4 & =2 k+2 \\ 2 k & =-6 \Rightarrow k=-3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of the quadratic equation $a x^2+b x+c=0$. Observe the lists given below
The correct match of List-I from List-II is (i) (ii) (iii) (iv)
questions.solution:
Using the condition that the roots of $a x^2+b x+c=0$ may be in the ratio $m: n$ is $m n b^2=a c(m+n)^2$.
(i) If the roots are $\alpha=\beta$, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha \cdot \alpha b^2 & =a c(\alpha+\alpha)^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad b^2 & =4 a c
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad b^2=4 a c \\
& \text { (ii) If the roots are } \alpha=2 \beta \text {, then } \\
& \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \beta \cdot 2 \beta b^2=a c(\beta+2 \beta)^2 \\
& 2 b^2=9 a c
\end{aligned}
$$
(iii) If the roots are $\alpha=3 \beta$, then
$$
\Rightarrow \quad \begin{aligned}
\beta \cdot 3 \beta b^2 & =a c(\beta+3 \beta)^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 3 b^2 & =16 a c
\end{aligned}
$$
(iv) If the roots are $\alpha=\beta^2$, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(a^2 c\right)^{\frac{1}{2+1}}+\left(a c^2\right)^{\frac{1}{2+1}}=-b \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \left(a^2 c\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}+\left(a c^2\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}=-b
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, option (d) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math><mi>α</mi></math> and <math><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi></math> are roots of the equation <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> then the value of <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
x2+5x-6=0
x2+5x-6=0
x2+6x-x-6=0
xx+6-1x+6=0
x-1x+6=0
x=1, nbsp;x≠-6 (Since modulus can not be giving negative values)
∴  x=1
∴  x=± 1
α=1,β=-1
∴   tan-1⁡α-tan-1⁡β=tan-1⁡1-tan-1⁡-1
=π4--π4
=π2 = π 2

questions.q: To remove the second term of the equation $x^4-8 x^3+x^2-x+3=0$, diminish the roots of the equation by
questions.solution:
We have, $x^4-8 x^3+x^2-x+3=0$
$$
h=-\frac{\text { coefficient of } x^3}{\text { number of roots }}=\frac{-(-8)}{4}=2
$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\sqrt{9 x^2+6 x+1} < (2-x)$, then:
questions.solution:
$\sqrt{9 x^2+6 x+1} < (2-x)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{(3 x+1)^2} < (2-x)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \pm(3 x+1) < 2-x$
Taking + ve sign
$3 x+1 < 2-x$
$\Rightarrow \quad x < \frac{1}{4}$
Taking -ve sign
$-3 x-1 < 2-x$
$\Rightarrow \quad-2 x < 3$
$\Rightarrow \quad x>-\frac{3}{2}$
$\therefore \quad x \in\left(-\frac{3}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The biquadratic equation, two of whose roots are $1+i, 1-\sqrt{2}$, is
questions.solution:
When $1+i, 1-i$ are the roots, then
Sum of the roots $=1+i+1-i=2$
and product of the roots $=1+1=2$
The equation is $x^2-2 x+2=0$
When $1-\sqrt{2}, 1+\sqrt{2}$ are the roots, then
Sum of the roots $=1-\sqrt{2}+1+\sqrt{2}=2$
Product of the roots $=1-2=-1$
The equation is $x^2-2 x-1=0$
The bi-quadratic equation is
$\begin{array}{r}\left(x^2-2 x+2\right)\left(x^2-2 x-1\right)=0 \\ \left(x^4-(2+2) x^3=(-1+4+2) x^2\right. \\ =(2-4) x-2=0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad x^4-4 x^3+5 x^2-2 x-2=0\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

$A M \text { of } a, b=34$
$a+b=68$
GM of $a, b,=\pm 6$
$\begin{aligned} a, b \rightarrow \text { roots } \rightarrow \quad a b &=256 \\ x^{2}-68 x+256 &=0 \end{aligned}$

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><msup><mn>3</mn><mi>n</mi></msup></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><msup><mn>3</mn><mi>n</mi></msup></msup></math> is divisible by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>,</mo></math> if
questions.solution:
Let A=x3n+y3n 

Put n=0, then

A=x30+y30=x+y

Hence, it is divisible by x+y.

Let this be true for some natural number n=k, then

A=x3k+y3k is divisible by x+y

⇒x3k+y3k=m(x+y),m∈Z ...(1).

Now, for n=k+1,

A=x3k+1+y3k+1

=x3k·3+y3k·3

=x3k3+y3k3

=x3k+y3kx3k2+y3k2-x3k·y3k (using a3+b3=a+ba2-ab+b2

=m(x+y)x3k2+y3k2-x3k·y3k (using (1))

⇒x3k+1+y3k+1 is divisible by x+y for all n≥0,n∈Z.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is a polynomial of degree four with the leading coefficient one satisfying <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mfrac><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>5</mn></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>4</mn></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mo>·</mo></mfenced></math> represents the greatest integer function) is equal to
questions.solution:
The roots of fx−x=0 are 1, 2 and 3.

So, we get,

fx−x=x−1x−2x−3x−a

For x=-1, we get,

f−1+1=−2−3−4−1−a=241+a

For x=5, we get,

f5−5=4·3·25−a=245−a

f-1+f5=23+24a+125-24a=148

For x=0, we get,

f0-0=-1-2-3-a=6a

For x=4, we get,

f4-4=3·2·1·4-a=24-6a

f0+f4=28

f-1+f5f0+f4=14828

f-1+f5f0+f4=5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let \(f(x)\) be a polynomial and \(a, b\) be distinct real numbers. Then the remainder in the division of \(f(x)\) by \((x-a)(x-b)\) is
questions.solution:
Let \(f(x)=(x-a)(x-b) \cdot q(x)+r(x)\)
Let \(r(x)=\alpha x+\beta \quad[\because \operatorname{deg} r(x)  < \operatorname{deg}\). of divisor]
\(\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad f(x) & =(x-a)(x-b) \cdot q(x)+\alpha x+\beta \\
f(a) & =\alpha a+\beta \quad \ldots (i) \\
f(b) & =\alpha b+\beta \quad \ldots (ii) 
\end{aligned}\)
Subtract Eqs. (i) from (ii)
\(\begin{aligned}
f(a)-f(b) & =\alpha(a-b) \\
\alpha & =\frac{f(a)-f(b)}{a-b} \\
\alpha & =\frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}
\end{aligned}\)
Put, \(\alpha\) in Eq. (i)
\(\begin{aligned}
f(a) & =\left(\frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}\right) \times a+\beta \\
f(a)[b-a] & =a f(b)-a f(a)+\beta(b-a) \\
b f(a)-a f(a) & =a f(b)-a f(a)+\beta(b-a) \\
\beta & =\frac{b f(a)-a f(b)}{(b-a)} \\
r(x) & =\alpha x+\beta \\
& =\frac{f(b)-f(a) x}{b-a}+\frac{b f(a)-a f(b)}{(b-a)} \\
& =\frac{x f(b)-x f(a)+b f(a)-a f(b)}{b-a} \\
r(x) & =\frac{(x-a) f(b)+(b-x) f(a)}{b-a} \\
r(x) & =\frac{(x-a) f(b)-(x-b) f(a)}{b-a}
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If $x=\sqrt{\frac{2+\sqrt{3}}{2-\sqrt{3}}}$, then $x^2(x-4)^2$ is equal to :
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of all real values of <math><mi>x</mi></math> satisfying the equation <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>60</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
The given equation will be true in 3 cases.

Case 1: when x2-5x+5=1  

⇒x2-5x+4=0

⇒x=1, 4

Case 2: when x2+4x-60=0

⇒x=6, -10

Case 3: when x2-5x+5=-1 and x2+4x-60∈ even integers

Now, x2-5x+5=-1    

⇒x=2, 3

Only x=2 satisfies the given condition,

Hence, sum of all real values of x is 1+4+6-10+2=3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) be the roots of the equation \(p x^2+q x+r=0, p \neq 0\). If \(p, q, r\) are in AP and \(\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta}=4\), then the value of \(|\alpha-\beta|\) is
questions.solution:
Since, \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are roots of quadratic equation \(p x^2+q x+r=0, p \neq 0\) and \(p, q, r\) are in AP such that
\(\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta}=4\) ...(i)
Let the \(p=q-d\) and \(r=q+d\), where \(d\) is the common difference of AP
\(\therefore\) Sum of the roots \(\alpha+\beta=\frac{-q}{q-d}\)
and product of the roots \(\alpha \beta=\frac{q+d}{q-d}\)
\(\because\) from Eq. (i), we have
\(\frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha \beta}=4 \Rightarrow \frac{-q}{q+d}=4 \Rightarrow 5 q+4 d=0\) ...(ii)
Now, \(|\alpha-\beta|=\sqrt{(\alpha+\beta)^2-4 \alpha \beta}\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =\sqrt{\frac{q^2}{(q-d)^2}-4 \frac{q+d}{q-d}}=\sqrt{\frac{q^2-4\left(q^2-d^2\right)}{(q-d)^2}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{4 d^2-3 q^2}{(q-d)^2}}=\sqrt{\frac{4\left(-\frac{5}{4} q\right)^2-3 q^2}{\left(q+\frac{5}{4} q\right)^2}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{\frac{25}{4}-3}{\left(\frac{9}{4}\right)^2}}=\frac{2}{9} \sqrt{13}
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^2+a x+b$, where $a, b \in R$. If $f(x)=0$ has all its roots imaginary, then the roots of $f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ are
questions.solution:
Given, $f(x)=x^2+a x+b$ has imaginary roots. $\therefore$ Discriminant, $D < 0 \Rightarrow a^2-4 b < 0$ Now, $\begin{aligned} f^{\prime}(x) & =2 x+a \\ f^{\prime \prime}(x) & =2 \end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & x^2+a x+b+2 x+a+2=0 \\ \Rightarrow & x^2+(a+2) x+b+a+2=0\end{array}$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad x=\frac{-(a+2) \pm \sqrt{(a+2)^2-4(a+b+2)}}{2} \\ & =\frac{-(a+2) \pm \sqrt{a^2-4 b-4}}{2} \\ & \text { Since, } \quad a^2-4 b < 0 \\ & \therefore \quad a^2-4 b-4 < 0 \\ & \end{aligned}$ Hence, Eq. (i) has imaginary roots.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ are the roots of the equation $x^3-6 x^2+11 x-6=0$ and if $a=\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2$, $b=\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha$ and $c=(\alpha+\beta)(\beta+\gamma)(\gamma+\alpha)$, then the correct inequality among the following is
questions.solution: Given equation $x^3-6 x^2+11 x-6=0$ has the roots $\alpha, \beta \gamma$. Given, $\quad a=\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2$ ...(i) $b=\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha$ ...(ii) $c=(\alpha+\beta)(\beta+\gamma)(\gamma+\alpha)$ ...(iii) In cubic equation the sum of the roots $\alpha+\beta+\gamma=-\left(\frac{-6}{1}\right)=6$ $\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha=\left(\frac{11}{1}\right)=11$ product of the roots $\alpha \cdot \beta \cdot \gamma=-\left(\frac{-6}{1}\right)=6$ From Eq. (ii), $b=11$ From Eq. (i), $a=\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2$ $\Rightarrow \quad a=(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)^2-2(\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha)$ $\Rightarrow \quad a=(6)^2-2(11) \Rightarrow 36-22$ $\Rightarrow \quad a=14$ From Eq. (iii) $c=(\alpha+\beta)(\beta+\gamma)(\gamma+\alpha)$ $=\left(\alpha \beta+\beta^2+\alpha \gamma+\beta \gamma\right)(\gamma+\alpha)$ $=\alpha \beta \gamma+\beta^2 \gamma+\alpha \gamma^2+\beta \gamma^2+\alpha^2 \beta$ $+\alpha \beta^2+\alpha^2 \gamma+\alpha \beta \gamma$ $\Rightarrow c=[(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)-\gamma][(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)-\alpha]$ $[(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)-\beta]$ $=(6-\gamma)(6-\alpha)(6-\beta)$ $=(36-6 \gamma-6 \alpha+\alpha \gamma)(6-\beta)$ $=216-36 \gamma-36 \alpha+6 \alpha \gamma-36 \beta+6 \gamma \beta$ $+6 \alpha \beta-\alpha \beta \gamma$ $=216-\alpha \beta \gamma+6(\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha)-36(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)$ $=216-6+6(11)-36(6)$ $=210+66-216=60$ Hence, $\quad c=60$ $\Rightarrow \quad b < a < c$Alternate method Given equation is $x^3-6 x^2+11 x-6=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)=0$ The roots of this equation are 1,2 , and 3 . Let $\alpha=1, \beta=2, \gamma=3$ Now, $\quad a=\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2$ $=1+4+9=14$ $b=\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha$ $=2+6+3=11$ $c=(\alpha+\beta)(\beta+\gamma)(\gamma+\alpha)$ $=3 \cdot 5 \cdot 4=60$ From the values of $a, b$ and $c$ it is clear that $b < a < c$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of $x^2+p x+q=0$, then the values of $\alpha^3+\beta^3$ and $\alpha^4+\alpha^2 \beta^2+\beta^4$ are respectively ...... and ......
options: $\left(3 p q-p^3\right)$ and $\left(p^4-3 p^2 q+3 q^2\right)$
questions.solution:
Since $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are roots of equation
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2+p x+q=0, \text { so } \\
& \alpha+\beta=-p \text { and } \alpha \beta=q
\end{aligned}
$$

As we know that, $\alpha^3+\beta^3=(\alpha+\beta)\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2-\alpha \beta\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =(\alpha+\beta)\left[(\alpha+\beta)^2-3 \alpha \beta\right] \\
& =(-p)\left[p^2-3 q\right]=3 p q-p^3 \\
\text { and } & \alpha^4+\alpha^2 \beta^2+\beta^4 \\
& =\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2\right)^2-\alpha^2 \beta^2=\left[(\alpha+\beta)^2-2 \alpha \beta\right]^2-(\alpha \beta)^2 \\
& =\left((-p)^2-2 q\right)^2-q^2 \\
& =\left(p^2-2 q\right)^2-q^2=\left(p^2-2 q-q\right)\left(p^2-2 q+q\right) \\
& =\left(p^2-q\right)\left(p^2-3 q\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The quadratic equation <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has both roots complex for all <math><mi>θ</mi></math> lying in the interval
questions.solution:
D=41-sin⁡θ2+41-sin⁡θ⋅3sin⁡θ<0
⇒ 41-sin⁡θ1-sin⁡θ+3sin⁡θ<0
⇒ 41-sin⁡θ1+2sin⁡θ<0
⇒ 8sin⁡θ-1sin⁡θ+12>0
⇒ sin⁡θ<-12 or sin⁡θ>1
sin⁡θ<-12⇒θ∈7π6,11π6

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $E_1: a+b+c=0, \quad$ if 1 is a root of $a x^2+b x+c=0, \quad E_2: b^2-a^2=2 a c$, if $\sin \theta$, $\cos \theta$ are the roots of $a x^2+b x+c=0$ Which of the following is true?
questions.solution:
Given that, 1 is a root of $a x^2+b x+c=0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad a+b+c=0 \\
& \therefore \quad E_1: a+b+c=0 \text { is true. }
\end{aligned}$
Since $\cos \theta, \sin \theta$ are the roots of
$\begin{aligned}
& a x^2+b x+c=0 \\
\therefore \quad & \sin \theta+\cos \theta=-\frac{b}{a} \\
\text { and } \sin \theta \cos \theta= & \frac{c}{a}
\end{aligned}$
On squaring both sides of equation (i)
$\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& (\sin \theta+\cos \theta)^2 & =\frac{b^2}{a^2} \\
\Rightarrow \sin ^2 \theta+\cos ^2 \theta+2 \sin \theta \cos \theta & =\frac{b^2}{a^2} \\
\Rightarrow & 1+2\left(\frac{c}{a}\right) & =\frac{b^2}{a^2} \\
\Rightarrow & 2 \cdot \frac{c}{a} & =\frac{b^2-a^2}{a^2} \\
\Rightarrow & -a^2+b^2 & =2 a c \\
\therefore & E_2: b^2-a^2 & =2 a c \text { is true }
\end{array}$
$\Rightarrow E_1$ and $E_2$ both are true.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $m_1$ and $m_2$ are the roots of the equation
$x^2+(\sqrt{3}+2) x+(\sqrt{3}-1)=0,$
then the area of the triangle formed by the lines $y=m_1 x, y=m_2 x$ and $y=c$
questions.solution:
Since, $m_1$ and $m_2$ are the roots of the equation $x^2+(\sqrt{3}+2) x+(\sqrt{3}-1)=0$
then $\quad m_1+m_2=-(\sqrt{3}+2)$,
$m_1 m_2=\sqrt{3}-1$
$\therefore m_1-m_2=\sqrt{\left(m_1+m_2\right)^2-4 m_1 m_2}$
$=\sqrt{(3+4+4 \sqrt{3}-4 \sqrt{3}+4)}$
$=\sqrt{11}$
and coordinates of the vertices of the given triangles are $(0,0),\left(c / m_1, c\right)$ and $\left(c / m_2, c\right)$. Hence, the required area of triangle
$=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ \frac{c}{m_1} & c & 1 \\ \frac{c}{m_2} & c & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2} c^2\left|\left(\frac{1}{m_1}-\frac{1}{m_2}\right)\right|$
$=\frac{1}{2} c^2 \frac{\left|m_2-m_1\right|}{m_1 m_2}$
$=\frac{1}{2} c^2 \frac{\sqrt{11}}{(\sqrt{3}-1)}$
$=\frac{1}{2} c^2 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{11} \cdot(\sqrt{3}+1)}{(\sqrt{3}-1)(\sqrt{3}+1)}$
$=\left(\frac{\sqrt{33}+\sqrt{11}}{4}\right) \cdot c^2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation $x^3+x-1=0$ has
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{f}(x)=x^3+x-1$
A root of $\mathrm{f}(x)$ exists, if $\mathrm{f}(x)=0$ for at least one value of $x$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{f}(0)=-1 < 0 \\
& \mathrm{f}(1)=1>0
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore \quad$ By intermediate value theorem, there has to be a point ' $c$ ' between 0 and 1 such that $\mathrm{f}(x)=0$.
$\therefore \quad$ The given equation has exactly one real root.
Alternate Method:
Let $\mathrm{f}(x)=x^3+x-1$
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore \quad \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+1 \\
\quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(x)>0 \quad \forall x \in \mathrm{R}
\end{array}
$$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(x)$ is an increasing function.
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}(x)$ intersects $\mathrm{X}$-axis at only one point.
$\therefore \quad$ The given equation has exactly one real root.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the bi-quadratic equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>16</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>22</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></math> as one of its roots, then which of the following is not a root of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>?</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Given, f(x)=x4+2x3-16x2-22x+7=0

∵ 2+3 is one of the roots of fx=0

∴ 2-3 is also one of the roots of fx=0

Let us assume the other two roots of the fx=0 are α, β

⇒ Sum of roots=-2

⇒α+β+2+3+2-3=-2

⇒α+β=-6 …i

And product of roots=7

⇒αβ2+32-3=7

⇒αβ=7 …ii

Put β=7α in equation i, we get

⇒α2+6α+7=0

⇒α=-6±36-282=-3±2

Put value of α in equation ii, we get

β=-3∓2

∴ Roots of the fx=0 are 2+3, 2-3, -3+2, -3-2.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi>α</mi></math> and <math><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></math> are in arithmetic progression and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi></math> are in harmonic progression, then the value of <math><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
1, α+β,αβ are in A.P. ⇒1,-ba,ca are in A.P.
⇒1+ca=-2ba⇒a+c+2b=0 … 1
1α,12,1β are in A.P. ⇒1α+1β=1⇒α+β=αβ
⇒-ba=ca⇒b+c=0 … 2
From 1 & 2 we get,
 a=-b=c
⇒α,β are roots of equation x2-x+1=0
Now, α2+β2-2α2β22α2+β2=12-(αβ)2(α+β)2-2αβ
=12-1212-21=12+1=1.5

questions.q: $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ are the roots of the equation $x^3-10 x^2+7 x+8=0$. Match the following and choose the correct answer.
questions.solution:
Since α, β and γ are the roots of the equation
On squaring equation (i) both sides, we get

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Again now, } \frac{\alpha}{\beta \gamma}+\frac{\beta}{\gamma \alpha}+\frac{\gamma}{\alpha \beta} \\ & =\frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2}{\alpha \beta \gamma}=\frac{86}{-8} \quad \text { [from Eqs (iii) and (iv)] } \\ & =-\frac{43}{4}\end{aligned}$ From the above discussion we see that option (3) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation $a x^2+b x+c=0$ and, if $p x^2+q x+r=0$ has roots $\frac{1-\alpha}{\alpha}$ and $\frac{1-\beta}{\beta}$, then $r$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Since, $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of the equation $a x^2+b x+c=0$.
$\therefore \quad \alpha+\beta=-\frac{b}{a} \quad \alpha \beta=\frac{c}{a}$
The quadratic equation whose roots are $\frac{1-\alpha}{\alpha}$ and $\frac{1-\beta}{\beta}$, is
$\begin{aligned} & x^2-\left(\frac{1-\alpha}{\alpha}+\frac{1-\beta}{\beta}\right) x+\frac{1-\alpha}{\alpha} \cdot \frac{1-\beta}{\beta}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-\left(\frac{\beta-\alpha \beta+\alpha-\alpha \beta}{\alpha \beta}\right) x \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-\left(\frac{-\frac{b}{a}-2 \frac{c}{a}}{\frac{c}{a}}\right) x+\frac{1-\left(-\frac{b}{a}\right)+\frac{c}{a}}{\frac{c}{a}}=0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow x^2-\left(\frac{-\frac{b}{a}-2 \frac{c}{a}}{\frac{c}{a}}\right) x+\frac{1-\left(-\frac{b}{a}\right)+\frac{c}{a}}{\frac{c}{a}}=0$
$\{$ from Eq. (i)\}
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow x^2-\frac{(-b-2 c) x}{c}+\frac{a+b+c}{c}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow c x^2+(b+2 c) x+(a+b+c)=0\end{aligned}$
On comparing with $p x^2+q x+r=0$, we get $r=a+b+c$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of the roots of the equation,<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><mo>|</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mo>-</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><mo>|</mo><mo> </mo><mo>-</mo><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></math> , is
questions.solution:
Given Equation : x2 + |2x - 3| - 4 = 0

x < 3 2 ⇒ x 2 - 2x - 3 - 4 = 0

⇒ x2 - 2x - 1 = 0

Roots α , β = 2 ± 4 + 4 2 = 1 ± 2

root 1 + 2 > 3 2 , hence rejected

⇒ root accepted 1 - 2

x ≥ 3 2 ⇒ x 2 + 2x - 3 - 4 = 0

⇒ x2 + 2x - 7 = 0

Roots γ , δ = - 2 ± 4 + 4 × 7 2 - 1 ± 2 2

root - 1 - 2 2 < 3 2 , hence rejected

⇒ root accepted - 1 + 2 2

Sum of roots = 1 - 2 + - 1 + 2 2 = 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Solve \((8-t)^2 < \left(t^2-3 t-10\right)\)
questions.solution:
It is given that,
\(\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& & (8-t)^2  < t^2-3 t-10 \\
\Rightarrow & 64-16 t+t^2 < t^2-3 t-10 \\
\Rightarrow & 13 t > 74 \\
\Rightarrow & t > \frac{74}{13} \\
\Rightarrow & t \in\left(\frac{74}{13}, \infty\right)
\end{array}\)
Hence, option (b) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The number of real roots of the equation, <math><msup><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>e</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is:
questions.solution:
Let ex=t∈0,∞
Given equation
t4+t3-4t2+t+1=0
t2+t-4+1t+1t2=0
t2+1t2+t+1t-4=0
Let t+1t=α
α2-2+α-4=0
α2+α-6=0
α2+α-6=0
α=-3,2⇒α=2⇒ex+e-x=2
x=0 only solutions

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
We have,
$(20)^{2-3 x^2}=(40 \sqrt{5})^{3 x^2-2}$
On taking log on both sides, we get
$\left(2-3 x^2\right) \log 20=\left(3 x^2-2\right) \log 40 \sqrt{5}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left(2-3 x^2\right)[2 \log 2+\log 5] \\ & =\left(3 x^2-2\right)\left(3 \log 2+\frac{3}{2} \log 5\right) \\ & \Rightarrow\left(3 x^2-2\right)\left[3 \log 2+\frac{3}{2} \log 5+2 \log 2+\log 5\right]=0 \\ & \Rightarrow\left(3 x^2-2\right)=0 \Rightarrow x^2=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow x= \pm \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ are the roots of $x^3+2 x^2-3 x-1=0$ then $\alpha^{-2}+\beta^{-2}+\gamma^{-2}=$
questions.solution:
If $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ are the roots of $x^3+2 x^2-3 x-1=0$ then $\alpha^{-2}+\beta^{-2}+\gamma^{-2}=$

On squaring equation (ii), we get $\begin{aligned} & \alpha^2 \beta^2+\beta^2 \gamma^2+\gamma^2 \alpha^2+2 \alpha \beta \gamma(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)=9 \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha^2 \beta^2+\beta^2 \gamma^2+\gamma^2 \alpha^2=9-2(1)(-2)=13 \end{aligned}$ Now $\begin{aligned} & \alpha^{-2}+\beta^{-2}+\gamma^{-2}=\frac{\beta^2 \gamma^2+\gamma^2 \alpha^2+\alpha^2 \beta^2}{(\alpha \beta \gamma)^2} \\ & \alpha^{-2}+\beta^{-2}+\gamma^{-2}=\frac{13}{1}=13 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is a factor of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mi>p</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi></math> , then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>q</mi></math> are
questions.solution:
x2- 3x+2=0 ∵ x=1, 2

Since x2- 3x+2 is a factor of x4- px2+q , the roots 1,2 are also roots of x4- px2+q=0

1- p+q=0 and 16- 4p+q=0

Solving, we get p=5, q=4

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> be roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>s</mi></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>18</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo><</mo><mi>q</mi><mo><</mo><mi>r</mi><mo><</mo><mi>s</mi></math> are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> are
questions.solution:
Let the four numbers in A.P. be

p=a-3d, q=a-d, r=a+d, s=a+3d.

Now, p and q are the roots of the quadratic equation x2-2x+A=0.

Therefore, using the sum and product of roots of a quadratic equation, we get

p+q=2   …i

pq=A   …ii

And, r,s are the roots of the quadratic equation x2-18x+B=0.

Therefore,

r+s=18   …iii

rs=B   …iv

By i and iii, we have

p+q+r+s=4a=20

⇒a=5

Now,

p+q=2

⇒10-4d=2

⇒d=2.

Also, 

r+s=18

⇒10+4d=18

⇒d=2.

Hence, the numbers are -1, 3, 7, 11.

Therefore, pq=A=-3, rs=B=77.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
